description: A clean web app that extracts transcripts from YouTube videos and podcasts, then generates structured AI summaries. Free tier: 3 per month. Pro: $9/month unlimited.

milestones.task: Competitor research — study the top 5 transcript tools, find their weaknesses, and define your unique angle
milestones.task_prompt:
I am building a SaaS called Bell Transcript — a YouTube and podcast transcript + summary tool. I need you to help me research the market before I start building.

Please research and give me a detailed breakdown of:
1. The top 5 existing YouTube/podcast transcript tools (include: Otter.ai, Descript, Tactiq, NoteGPT, and one more you find). For each: what they charge, what their free tier includes, what their most common customer complaints are, what they do well
2. Based on the complaints and gaps, what are the 3 biggest opportunities for a new tool to be better?
3. What should my unique angle be — what can Bell Transcript do that others don't, or do cheaper/simpler?
4. Who is my ideal customer? (be specific — job title, use case, how often they need this)
5. What features should be in Version 1 (MVP) vs saved for later?
6. What should I charge? Suggest a pricing model with specific numbers.

Give me real, specific answers — not generic advice. I want to start building next week.

milestones.task: Define MVP features and build the core transcript extraction script (foundation from Week 4 project)
Build a simple web interface using Flask so users can paste a YouTube URL and get a summary in the browser
Add user accounts — sign up, log in, track how many transcripts each user has used
Add Stripe payment integration for the $9/month Pro plan
Deploy to the internet using a free hosting service, get a domain name, write the landing page
Get first 10 users — post in 5 relevant Reddit communities, reach out to 10 YouTubers directly
First revenue review — how many free users, how many paid, what do they say, what to improve
